Plastic (PET).notes: PET (1) and HDPE (2) plastics are most widely accepted.

prep: Check the resin code (▲ symbol) on the bottom
Plastic.notes: Not all plastics are recyclable everywhere — check local guidelines.

Glass.notes: Ceramics, mirrors, and window glass are usually NOT accepted.

prep: You may crush it to save space
Aluminum.notes: Aluminum cans are among the most valuable recyclables — always recycle.

Metal.notes: Scrap metal may need a separate drop-off point.

prep: Remove tape and staples if possible
Cardboard.notes: Pizza boxes with heavy grease are often rejected — check locally.

Paper.notes: Shredded paper may need to be bagged separately.

Organic.notes: Meat and dairy may not be accepted in all compost programs — check locally.

prep: Do NOT place in regular bins
Find a certified e-waste collection point
E-Waste.notes: Many retailers offer take-back programs for electronic devices.

prep: Place in a textile collection bin or donate
Textile.notes: Wearable clothes should be donated before recycling.

prep: Cannot be further sorted — place in residual waste
Mixed Waste.notes: Try to identify components that can be separated and recycled individually.

Ceramic.notes: Ceramics cannot be processed with glass recycling.
